steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank falls inside a noun phrase that sets up the end of the first independent clause: "the site with the highest ______". The second independent clause follows immediately: "it had shifted to 16.2°C by 8:30 p.m." Two independent clauses must be separated by a period, semicolon, or a comma plus coordinating conjunction — not by a comma alone or nothing at all.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — elevation: No punctuation between the two independent clauses — a run-on sentence. ✗
  • Choice B — elevation, and: Completes the noun phrase with "elevation," then joins the two independent clauses with a comma plus the coordinating conjunction and. Grammatically correct. ✓
  • Choice C — elevation and: Uses the conjunction and without the required comma before it when joining two independent clauses. ✗
  • Choice D — elevation,: Places only a comma between two independent clauses — a comma splice. ✗
B is correct.
